question_text: A household uses a 2.4 kW electric shower for 8 minutes per day. Electricity costs 28p per kWh. Calculate the total cost of using this shower for 30 days.
explanation: First, convert 8 minutes to hours: 8 ÷ 60 = 0.133 hours. Energy per day = 2.4 kW × 0.133 h = 0.32 kWh. Energy for 30 days = 0.32 × 30 = 9.6 kWh. Cost = 9.6 kWh × 28p = 268.8p = £2.69. (Common error: students may forget to convert minutes to hours, or multiply by the wrong time period.)
